proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with different mean pfs_months than male.
Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.766, p~0; age and PFS are strongly positively correlated.
Mean PFS differs by 0.006 months by sex; t-test p=0.77 (not significant).
Pearson r=-0.352, p~0; worse ECOG is significantly associated with shorter PFS.
Stage IV patients have PFS lower by 1.35 months (p~0).
Right-sided primaries have PFS lower by 0.305 months (p=1.0e-49).

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS-wt.
nras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than NRAS-wt.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF-wt.
msi_high=1 is associated with different pfs_months than MSS.
her2_amplified=1 is associated with different pfs_months than HER2-neg.
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with different pfs_months than non-fusion.

analyses.result_summary: KRAS-mutant PFS shorter by 0.327 months (p=1.9e-58).
NRAS-mutant PFS LONGER by 0.221 months (p=7e-4) -- opposite of hypothesis direction.
BRAF-V600E PFS shorter by 0.228 months (p=3.5e-7).
MSI-H PFS not meaningfully different from MSS (-0.019 mo, p=0.68).
HER2-amp PFS not different (-0.056 mo, p=0.36).
NTRK-fusion PFS not different (+0.040 mo, p=0.79).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: r=+0.102, p=3e-115; higher albumin associated with longer PFS.
r=-0.123, p=2e-167; weight loss inversely associated with PFS.
r=-0.038, p=3e-17; higher CEA modestly associated with shorter PFS.
r=-0.011, p=0.013; LDH only weakly inversely related to PFS.
r=-0.008, p=0.065; not significant at 0.05.
r=+0.005, p=0.29; no association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_bevacizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS lower on cetuximab by 0.038 mo overall (p=0.088, NS).
Mean PFS lower on bevacizumab by 0.019 mo (p=0.35, NS).
Mean PFS slightly higher on pembrolizumab (+0.005 mo, p=0.86, NS).
Mean PFS slightly higher on encorafenib (+0.005 mo, p=0.87, NS).
Mean PFS lower on tras/tuc by 0.044 mo (p=0.24, NS).
Mean PFS HIGHER on regorafenib by 0.97 months (p=1.7e-216) -- strong main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs by kras_mutation status (cetuximab benefit larger in KRAS-wt).
Effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs by nras_mutation status (cetuximab benefit larger in NRAS-wt).
Effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs by braf_v600e status (cetuximab benefit larger in BRAF-wt).
Effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs by right_sided_primary status (cetuximab benefit larger in left-sided).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.020, p=0.67. No detectable cetuximab x KRAS interaction.
Interaction coef -0.007, p=0.96. No interaction.
Interaction coef -0.026, p=0.81. No interaction.
Interaction coef +0.026, p=0.58. No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months within RAS/BRAF-wt patients (kras=0 & nras=0 & braf=0).
treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months within RAS/BRAF-wt left-sided patients.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab effect in RAS/BRAF-wt: -0.045 mo (n+=7691 vs n-=17633), p=0.17. No benefit.
Cetuximab effect in RAS/BRAF-wt left-sided: -0.067 mo (n+=5067 vs n-=11376), p=0.13. No benefit; trend slightly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by msi_high (benefit larger in MSI-H).
treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months within msi_high=1 subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.002, p=0.99. No pembrolizumab x MSI interaction.
Pembrolizumab in MSI-H: +0.007 mo (n+=396 vs n-=2117), p=0.96. No detectable benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months differs by braf_v600e (benefit larger in BRAF-V600E).
treatment_encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months within braf_v600e=1 subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.141, p=0.38. No significant interaction.
Encorafenib in BRAF-V600E: -0.129 mo (n+=242 vs n-=2030), p=0.33. No detectable benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months differs by her2_amplified (benefit larger in HER2-amp).
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months within her2_amplified=1 subgroup.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months within HER2-amp & RAS-wt subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.063, p=0.78. No detectable interaction.
Tras/tuc in HER2-amp: +0.017 mo (n+=120 vs n-=1384), p=0.93. No benefit.
Tras/tuc in HER2-amp & RAS-wt: +0.052 mo (n+=74 vs n-=771), p=0.86. No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs by stage_iv.
Effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs by braf_v600e.
treatment_bevacizumab is associated with longer pfs_months within stage_iv=1 subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.004, p=0.93. No interaction.
Interaction coef -0.228, p=0.022. Marginal heterogeneity but tiny absolute effect.
Bev in stage IV: -0.019 mo (n+=12322 vs n-=15159), p=0.48. No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is larger in KRAS-wt than KRAS-mutant (interaction).
treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months in kras_mutation=0 subgroup; effect is absent in kras_mutation=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -1.629, p=8e-226. Massive heterogeneity: regorafenib benefit suppressed by KRAS mutation.
Regorafenib in KRAS-wt: +1.66 mo (p=6e-293). In KRAS-mut: +0.027 mo (p=0.46). KRAS-wt drives the entire main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is larger in left-sided primaries than right-sided (interaction).
treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months in right_sided_primary=0; effect absent in right_sided_primary=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -1.454, p=4e-168. Right-sided suppresses regorafenib benefit.
Regorafenib in left-sided: +1.48 mo (p=4e-274). In right-sided: +0.026 mo (p=0.52).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is larger in BRAF-wt than BRAF-V600E (interaction).
treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months in braf_v600e=0; effect absent in braf_v600e=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -1.183, p=6e-23. BRAF-V600E suppresses regorafenib benefit.
Regorafenib in BRAF-wt: +1.027 mo (p=5e-226). In BRAF-V600E: -0.156 mo (p=0.13).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs by nras_mutation.
treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months in NRAS-mutant patients (and in NRAS-wt).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.988, p=1.2e-11. Effect is LARGER in NRAS-mut.
Regorafenib in NRAS-mut: +1.93 mo (n+=313 vs n-=1193, p=2.8e-21). In NRAS-wt: +0.94 mo. NRAS-mut does NOT eliminate benefit; it amplifies it.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the subgroup defined by kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND braf_v600e=0 (favorable), treatment_regorafenib substantially increases pfs_months; outside this subgroup the effect is null.

analyses.result_summary: Within favorable (KRAS-wt & left-sided & BRAF-wt; n=17,372): regorafenib +2.76 mo (n+=3461 vs n-=13911, p~0). Outside favorable: +0.030 mo (p=0.29). Joint interaction coef +2.73, p~0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment, age_years independently predicts longer pfs_months.
After adjustment, ecog_ps independently predicts shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, stage_iv independently predicts shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, albumin_g_dl independently predicts longer pfs_months.
After adjustment for all features, treatment_regorafenib independently predicts longer pfs_months.
After adjustment, alkaline_phosphatase, AST, ALT, sodium, potassium, calcium, creatinine, BUN, total_bilirubin are not independent predictors of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coef +0.176/yr, p~0.
Adjusted ECOG coef -1.171, p~0.
Adjusted stage_iv coef -1.362, p~0.
Adjusted albumin coef +0.469/(g/dL), p~0.
Adjusted regorafenib main coef +0.943, p~0.
AlkP, AST, ALT, sodium, potassium, calcium, creatinine, BUN, total bilirubin all p>0.05; no independent contribution. Model R^2=0.859.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable molecular/sidedness subgroup, the regorafenib pfs_months benefit decreases as cea_ng_ml rises.
Outside the favorable subgroup, cea_ng_ml does NOT modify the (already null) regorafenib effect.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.065 mo per ng/mL, p=7e-136. Benefit shrinks rapidly with rising CEA.
Interaction coef +0.0015, p=0.41. CEA does NOT modify regorafenib outside the favorable molecular/sidedness subgroup; the gating is multiplicative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, regorafenib increases pfs_months substantially when cea_ng_ml <= 5 ng/mL.
Within the favorable subgroup with cea_ng_ml > 5, regorafenib does NOT increase pfs_months.
Within the favorable subgroup, the regorafenib effect is essentially null in the highest CEA quartile (Q4: cea_ng_ml > ~10 ng/mL).

analyses.result_summary: n+=1556 vs n-=6478; mean diff -0.002 mo, p=0.97. Benefit completely absent.
Q4 (CEA 9.91-442): diff -0.017 mo, p=0.83. Effect absent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib substantially increases pfs_months only in the subgroup defined by ALL of: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml <= 5 ng/mL.
Outside that subgroup (any unfavorable predicate present), treatment_regorafenib does not increase pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest within KRAS-wt & left-sided & BRAF-wt & CEA<=5
analyses.result_summary: n+=1905 vs n-=7433; mean diff +5.01 months (p~0). Subgroup is roughly 19% of cohort.
Complement (any of KRAS-mut OR right-sided OR BRAF-V600E): n+=6561 vs n-=26067; mean diff +0.030 mo (p=0.29). Within favorable but CEA>5: diff -0.002 (p=0.97). All complements null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all binary features (sex, stage, sidedness, KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/MSI/HER2/NTRK), no feature significantly modifies the cetuximab pfs_months effect at p<0.05 after a full screen.
Within the RAS/BRAF-wt cetuximab-eligible subgroup, no continuous feature (age, ECOG, CEA, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin) modifies cetuximab effect at p<0.05.

analyses.code: Loop ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab * feature') for all binary features
analyses.result_summary: Smallest p among 9 cetuximab x feature interactions: cetuximab x msi_high p=0.115; all others p>0.2. No detectable cetuximab heterogeneity.

analyses.code: Loop ols within RAS/BRAF-wt across continuous features
analyses.result_summary: Smallest p: age_years (p=0.115). All others p>0.13. No cetuximab heterogeneity by continuous lab values.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No binary feature significantly modifies the pembrolizumab pfs_months effect (full screen across sex/stage/sidedness/KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/MSI/HER2/NTRK) in a way that yields a meaningful (>0.2 mo) subgroup benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Smallest p among 9 pembrolizumab x feature interactions: pembro x right_sided p=0.05 (coef -0.116); pembro x NRAS p=0.09; pembro x MSI p=0.99. No biomarker robustly modifies pembrolizumab. Even MSI-H subgroup mean diff is essentially 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No binary feature significantly modifies the encorafenib pfs_months effect at p<0.05 in a full screen.

analyses.result_summary: Smallest p: encorafenib x msi_high (coef -0.296, p=0.062); encorafenib x BRAF p=0.38. No binary feature reaches significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No binary feature significantly modifies the trastuzumab/tucatinib pfs_months effect in a full screen.

analyses.result_summary: Smallest p: tt x right_sided p=0.22 (coef +0.096); tt x HER2 p=0.78. No feature reaches significance; no detectable HER2-driven benefit even though that is the on-label indication.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No binary feature meaningfully modifies the bevacizumab pfs_months effect (only marginal BRAF interaction with very small absolute effect).

analyses.result_summary: Most significant: bev x BRAF coef -0.228 p=0.022 (BRAF-V600E reverses sign of an already-null effect); but absolute bevacizumab effect remains tiny (<0.1 mo) in every subgroup. No meaningful clinical benefit identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: The only treatment with a clinically meaningful pfs_months benefit in this cohort is treatment_regorafenib, and its benefit is concentrated in patients meeting ALL of: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml <= 5 ng/mL. Within that subgroup the mean PFS gain is ~5 months; outside it the mean PFS gain is statistically and clinically null.

FINAL: treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, and treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib show no clinically meaningful PFS benefit overall or within any biomarker- or feature-defined subgroup tested in this cohort.

FINAL prognostic drivers of pfs_months (independent of treatment): older age (longer PFS, +0.18 mo/yr adjusted), higher ECOG (shorter PFS), stage IV (shorter PFS), right-sided primary (shorter PFS), KRAS or BRAF mutation (shorter PFS), greater weight loss (shorter PFS), higher CEA (shorter PFS), higher LDH (shorter PFS), lower albumin (shorter PFS).

analyses.code: Final 5-feature subgroup test (KRAS-wt & NRAS-wt & BRAF-wt & left-sided & CEA<=5)
analyses.result_summary: n+=1905 vs n-=7433 within the subgroup; mean PFS diff +5.01 months (p~0). Outside the subgroup the effect is null (~0.03 mo, p=0.29). Subgroup ~19% of cohort.

Main effects: cetuximab -0.04 (p=0.09), bev -0.02 (p=0.35), pembro +0.005 (p=0.86), enco +0.005 (p=0.87), tt -0.04 (p=0.24). Heterogeneity screens: no biomarker subgroup yields a clinically meaningful (>0.2 mo) significant benefit for any of these five drugs.
All listed covariates significant at p<<0.001 in the multivariable model except msi_high, her2_amplified, ntrk_fusion, sex_female, NRAS, and most chemistry labs which were null.
